content: References: - Andy and Sandeep’s investment viewed as positive signal - Mentioned comparable models: - VRBO (US) - Stanza - Quintu - FastFox - [Housing.com/CommonFloor](http://housing.com/CommonFloor) **CV:** WM – non incremental points below. ?? Not compounding – feels very inside out. ?? Cannot sell – and this needs a ton of capital ?? All over the place in a prop that he has designed ?? unsure he will get hands dirty and build it out **TD:** **KK:** **NS:** **Spiky: **0 **EF/FF: **EO (insights of an FF) **Founder Archetype: **6 **FMF: **7 **Thesis: **6 ? No spike, inside out thinking. ? No baseline research from supply or demand side. ? Inarticulate, overcomplicated explanation of a straightforward business model. ? No unique insights being a potential EO. <u>**Meeting Notes**</u> **Team** - [Sahil](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sahilludhani/) (Co-founder) - SRCC grad (2009) - Ex-Zomato: International expansion (launched NZ, Malaysia, Indonesia, Philippines) - Led CloudKitchens & Zomato Gold verticals - Ex-Stanza: Led operations, sales strategy, and supply - [Ashutosh](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ashutosh-tandon/) (Co-founder) - SRCC grad (2012) - Ex-Zomato: Sales & global growth team - Ex-CRED: Built CRED Store - Scaled to 1.5M orders/month - 5-6M monthly users (50% of CRED users) **Problem** - Real estate rental market stuck in “classifieds 1.0” - High friction rental process for both owners & tenants - Large security deposits (4-6 months) blocking access - No standardized...

Final view 2026-01-09T10:28:00.000+05:30: 1. Trupti is the OG at CRED 1. TD had also emailed about this investment but we are late to it 1. Also, late in DeVC Fund I cycle to consider 1. They are selling to USA and neither is moving to USA 1. Next will be Z47 company 1. From @Aakash 1. Didn't get over the line given past context of them meandering through multiple thesis areas and co-founder mix. 1. Didnt meet the current (near end of fund cycle) bar on AI bets. 1. Trupthi is a great cred OG crew person, but both seemed non-committal to a full US shift and double down. 1. We should be tracking from Z for sure.

Current Product: AI Agent for ML Development: - Building “Emily” (MLE) - AI agent that replaces ML developers - Core value prop: One good ML developer + agent = work of 5 developers - Target market: Companies spending $2.5M+ on ML talent (5 developers) - Key differentiator vs AutoML: Can reason through ML problems and generate new strategies, not just hyperparameter search
heads up note 2025-08-29T12:43:00.000+05:30 by @Rahul Mathur: Hi team, Sharing the heads-up note for our investment in [Hexo AI](https://hexo.ai/). This is an AI agent which builds ML pipelines & workflows; it is similar to the coding Devin agent - acts like a member of your ML team DeVC is investing $50K as part of a $3M SAFE round @ $15M post money valuation (standard YC SAFE terms) Note: This is a toe-hold into the company; Cash & I are doing more work (e.g. BRCs, expert chats, network introductions & co-investor connects) & seek to form this round after meeting the founder in SF; we have got positive signal from Soumyadeep (DashReels, Z47 portfolio) and Sudarshan (Smallest AI, DeVC portfolio) **Founders** 1. [Kunal Bhatia](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kunalbhatia91/) - CEO 1. BITS ‘12 - MSc Economics graduate 1. 1.5 YOE as CoFo @ SuperLearn 1. Netflix-like learning platform, 100K users, shut down post-COVID when demand evaporated 1. 4.5 YOE as independent consultant on GovTech projects 1. Built grievance system processing 60M complaints for UP Chief Minister 1. [Vignesh Baskaran](https://www.linkedin.com...

Dhairen - First Meeting - 2026-02-25: Source: DT via SPC India. Founder is SPC fellow. DT Views: ++ Past track record - 2x founder, Bugle acquired by Acceldata ++ GTM wedge identified through company research platform (Empor) +? Acceldata refs needed to calibrate ?? Urgency and courage of conviction - why not put in own money and hire? ?? Real differentiation ?? Why isn't SPC leaning in here already --- SHAN'S BACKGROUND - 2x founder with strong technical background - 8 years at Google (Maps search, launched "One Today" product) - Built AI company Bugle (acquired by Acceldata during COVID) - Head of AI/ML at Acceldata for 1+ year post-acquisition PRODUCT EVOLUTION: EMPOR TO HEYFINN Started with Empor (company research website): - Built for personal investing research - 40k organic users, $0 marketing spend - Viral among portfolio managers globally - Compresses initial research time from half-day to 1 hour Discovered real pain point through customer conversations: - Portfolio managers need junior analyst capabilities - Typical junior analyst costs $150k-300k annually - Universe limitation: ~200 stocks vs 10k+ investable globally HEYFINN PRODUCT DETAILS Positioning: AI junior research analyst - 45 days old, 6 professional portfolio managers using it - 5 users outside India, including JP Morgan London manager - $10k annual pricing (customer wants $20k) Core capabilities: - Autonomous monitoring (earnings, management changes, analyst reports) - Adjacent stock investigation based ...

Ref from [Gaurav Shahlot](https://www.linkedin.com/in/gauravshahlot/) (CPO at Khatabook): <u>Context</u>: Gaurav hired Varun into Khatabook 1. he's got super strong product chops 1. really good at identifying and breaking down problem statements 1. Was good at talking to users & finding opportunities to move the needle on core metrics. 1. Extremely good at working with engineers and people in general. 1. I would totally trust him to build top notch products. 1. Was one of the stronger PMs in my team. 1. **The only callout from my side would be he gets excited by something new & interesting but could lose interest over time.** ---
Notes:: 1. Journey since last conversation 1. Decided to NOT pursue the BFSI x Indian market 1. Came to SFO 1. Worked on some Enterprise ideas - realized that there is no RTW 1. Now focusing on Consumer AI 1. What in Consumer AI? 1. Companion space 1. Approaching it from a proactive AI lens 1. Context & memory across apps (? how is unclear?) 1. Thinking about the accountability space e.g. go to the gym or do some work 1. Very early thoughts 1. Why come to SF? 1. Felt learnings were saturated 1. All VCs suggested that they move to SFO for learning 1. Fundraise situation 1. Raised ~$10K as an Angel round 1. Would spend ~80% of it over next 2 months 1. Looking to do a small $500K Pre-Seed 1. Action items 1. Get introduced to his father who is GCC head for Exxon Mobil in India 1. Introduce him to Riverline AI to beat some sense into the kids ---

Meeting Notes (17 Feb - Gurgaon Office) - 2026-02-26: TARUN'S BACKGROUND & POLICY BAZAAR JOURNEY: - 13 years in ecosystem, started with comparison websites and SEO/SEM - Built revenue models through Google AdSense, then booking engines - Set up Affle's partnership platform in India - worked with 500+ brands creating performance marketing and partnership models POLICY BAZAAR ACHIEVEMENTS: - Term insurance: Increased commissions from Rs 700 to Rs 3,400 per transaction in 1.5 years - Investment products: Built high-IRR products (15-18%) competing with mutual funds - Motor insurance: Created CURE model, became contribution positive - Health insurance: Developed "one crore health person" product with 15% additional premium - Key insight: One RM per customer dramatically improves NPS/CSAT and purchase control - Health persistency increased from 70-72% to current levels through dedicated RM - Cross-sell ratios improved from 1.4-1.5x to 2.6x transactions, 4x revenue HULP'S CORE CONCEPT & TARGET MARKET: - Personal assistance for modern families - "Can we take away all the pain in your life?" - Target: Parents with two kids not living with their own parents (dual-income households with Rs 70L+ income) - Subscription model at Rs 3,000-5,000/month - One PA managing 12 families initially, scaling to 25-27 families by year three - Architecture: PA as front-end with specialized desks (travel, maid coordination, government forms) in backend THREE CORE PRODUCT PILLARS FOR DAILY USAGE: 1. Meal...

Notes:: Dr Marcus Setup clinic and the firm about 5 years ago. Focus is health span and not life span: life span is for only the top echelon and low accessible to all. Health span is the focus where you create widespread impact/effects - Supplements have low barrier to entry and low regulation - Devices increasingly exploding in terms of market size; but increasingly commoditized - Diagnostics is high capex and continuous iteration - Clinics is also high capex - so didn't think about opening chain of clinics - Saw opportunity in data - wearables driving + diagnostic/blood based testing on the rise People are creating data but don't know what to do with it. Device agnostic centralized intelligence is a value prop. Building a purely digital first experience + partnered with 1mg thyrocare etc have integrated all devices: whoop, garmin etc etc Collect data into central system - scrub and process it - then build a clinical dashboard for end user "Man in the middle approach" - doc/expert validates the output of rule engine (intellectually notes "it's not ML yet broadly a rule engine") Positive surprise on hypothesis: willingness of people to try Sick care network fragility during pandemic has pushed people to try lifestyle first Increased willingness combined with social media has also created confusion Science and authenticity is the key invite driver: people see the difference between nutritionist vs a clinical practice "we are the only company in India (comparable in US peter in...

What are their views?** 1. 1. **What do refs say about the founder?** 1. 1. **What's the DeVC team views?** 1. 1. **What are the Collective signals?** 1. 1. **Why is this company likely to be special?** 1. 1. **Who can help? Experts / Venture Partners?** 1. 1. **Deal dynamics? Potential deal to do?** 1. ---

AI Notes:: This meeting was an investment evaluation call with Nirav, founder of Ignosis, an Account Aggregator (AA) orchestration and analytics platform in India's fintech sector. Company Background: - Nirav founded Ignosis in January 2022 after running Pyramid FinTech (IT services company) - He's selling Pyramid (~50 Cr valuation) to focus on Ignosis - Previously spent 12 years in New York working in fintech - Based in Ahmedabad with 30-person team (24 tech, 6 non-tech) - Co-founder Chintan handles tech while Nirav handles business - Hiring strategy: Recruit locally from colleges and groom young talent Product & Technology: - Provides AA orchestration across multiple Account Aggregators - Creates 20% improvement in AA conversion rates through intelligent routing - Core analytics layer categorizes transactions, identifies income, spending patterns - Offers white-labeled Personal Finance Management (PFM) solutions - Integrates deeply with clients' loan origination systems - Competitive advantage: Real-time categorization engine with continuous improvement Business Model & Traction: - 50+ clients with only one churn - ~15-16 crore annual revenue - Two main revenue streams: - Lending analytics (60% of revenue): ₹4-7 per fetch - PFM solutions (40% of revenue): ~₹1-1.5 per active user monthly - MobiKwik is largest customer (40L active users, ~₹40L monthly revenue) - Transaction-level margins around 70% - Profitable, but raising capital for potential AA license acquisition Competi...
